name: Loop 1: The four passes – 10 Pyrenean itineraries
name_original: Boucle 1 : Les quatre cols — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This first loop is one of the best known and most appreciated by all fans of road cycling in Ariège. From Tarascon-sur-Ariège, it has the particularity of crossing no less than four passes, which in the region are called ports (which in the Gascon language, translates as pass).

The landscapes are splendid, because you evolve in the very heart of the Regional Natural Park of the Ariège Pyrenees. The warm-up is gradual. We enter the hard when we leave the valley of Niaux at the level of Vicdessos and a little before Auzat. It is the small road of Suc which leads you after 12 kilometers of ascent to the port of Lers. A short descent follows to the Etang de Lers to quickly climb the Col d'Agnès which is reached after only eight kilometres.

The descent to Aulus-les-bains is a pure delight, because the road is winding to perfection and has many bends.

Arrived in this spa village, you are halfway through, take the opportunity to take a break. Its tranquility and the course of the Garbet are very pleasant.

The second part of the route presents less difficulty, because the passes are lower than the previous ones, but the distance and the fatigue help, they will certainly not be easy.

In addition to Aulus-les-Bains and Tarascon which are not lacking in charm, you pass close to the cave of Bédeilhac known for its monumental porch where you could land small light planes. After this loop, do not hesitate to visit this cavity, as well as the world-famous Niaux cave, famous for its parietal paintings and its excellent acoustics.

Shortly after the Col de Saraillé you will discover one of the essentials of Couserans. The Cominac stone barns here offer fantastic views of Mount Valier.
region_intro: Hiking around Tarascon-sur-Ariège offers access to the French Pyrenees, characterized by river valleys, dense woodlands, and mountain peaks. The region is situated at the confluence of the Ariège and Vicdessos rivers, providing varied terrain from valley floors to higher elevations. Hikers can explore limestone hills, oak groves, and traverse scenic passes that offer views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape. Best hiking trails around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège The most popular hiking route is Saint Paul's Chapel – Ussat Pass loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through forests and offers views over the Tarascon basin. Another top favourite among local hikers is Col de l'Ussat – Saint Paul's Chapel loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and provides vistas of the Pyrenean peaks and valleys. Local hikers also love the Castella Tour – Col de l'Ussat loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with over 3,400 feet (1,000 meters) of elevation gain, often completed in about 7 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège is defined by Pyrenean peaks, river valleys, and woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging excursions. The routes in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Tarascon-Sur-Ariège's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: Tarascon-Sur-Ariège offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking routes. These trails vary in difficulty, ranging from easy walks to more challenging excursions, ensuring there's something for every hiker to explore the Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: Yes, Tarascon-Sur-Ariège caters to all levels of hikers. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available, perfect for beginners or families looking for a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty. These trails often wind through woodlands and offer pleasant views without significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on hikes around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: The terrain around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège is highly varied, from picturesque river valleys at the confluence of the Ariège and Vicdessos rivers to dense woodlands and majestic Pyrenean peaks. You can expect elevation gains ranging from moderate climbs of around 400 meters to more challenging ascents exceeding 1,000 meters on longer routes, offering expansive views of the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the popular hikes around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège are circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6983088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Paul's Chapel – Ussat Pass loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular trail that takes you through forests and offers views over the Tarascon basin.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking trails in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège often lead to or pass by significant landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6240014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Sabart Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, or climb to the top of the Tour du Castella for panoramic views. The region is also known for its unique geological formations and natural features like the Col de l'Ussat, offering stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers, Tarascon-Sur-Ariège offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10535518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castella Tour – Col de l'Ussat loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 20 kilometers and involves more than 1,000 meters of ascent, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: The hiking routes in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse Pyrenean landscapes, the tranquil atmosphere of the woodlands, and the expansive views from scenic passes and peaks. The well-maintained trails and variety of options for different skill levels are also often highlighted.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: Many trails in the Tarascon-Sur-Ariège region are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the Pyrenean scenery with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage at trailheads.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Yes, many hikes in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège are renowned for their superb viewpoints. Trails leading to the Col de l'Ussat, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39216523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de l'Ussat – Saint Paul's Chapel loop from Tarascon-sur-Ariège&lt;/a&gt;, provide expansive vistas of the Pyrenean peaks and valleys. Additionally, a walk to the top of the Tour du Castella offers panoramic views of the town, river, and surrounding mountains.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: The warmer months, from spring through autumn, are generally ideal for hiking in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège. During this period, the weather is pleasant, and the trails are most accessible. Summer offers lush greenery and full access to higher elevations, while spring and autumn provide comfortable temperatures and beautiful seasonal colors. Winter can offer opportunities for snowshoeing or cross-country skiing, but many hiking trails may be covered in snow.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads from Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: Tarascon-Sur-Ariège serves as a good base for exploring the surrounding mountains. While some trailheads might be accessible directly from the town, for others, you might need to rely on local bus services or personal transport. It's recommended to check local transport schedules in advance, especially for more remote starting points, to plan your hike effectively.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Tarascon-Sur-Ariège?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Tarascon-Sur-Ariège, particularly near popular trailheads or in the town center. Many routes, especially circular ones, start directly from the town, where public parking options exist. For specific trailheads outside the town, designated parking areas are often provided, though it's always wise to arrive early during peak seasons.
tips: Lers pond is located in the Ariège Pyrenees, at 1,264 meters above sea level. It lies between Aulus-les-Bains to the southwest, Vicdessos to the southeast and Massat to the northwest.

The Col d’Agnes has a slight advantage over the Port de Lers in terms of beauty. In fact, the western driveway is a feast for the eyes, ranking at the forefront of all the wonderful passes of the Ariège. And the approach from Massat or the Port de Lers boasts beautiful views of the Etang de Lers. Thanks to the variety of passes in the area, the Col d’Agnes can be easily combined with others, apart from the already mentioned Col de Port.
This wild and unknown pass is located in the heart of the Massat region.
This pass is one of the best known in the Ariège Pyrenees in general, and in the L'Arize massif in particular.
